# Spec DB ↔ Part 4 Reconciliation
<!-- Generated by Block 3 A2 — 2026-04-19 -->
<!-- Sonnet analysis. Opus review required for any conflict escalations. -->

**Purpose:** Identify (a) Part 4 items without BPC coverage; (b) BPC slugs without Part 4 mapping; (c) field-level discrepancies.
**Scope:** 91 Part 4 items (excluding merged/absorbed); 70 active/stub BPC slugs.
**Method:** Item category × population code × BPC topic directory cross-reference. No BPC files loaded individually — reconciliation uses slug-registry + part04-item-index analytical mapping.

---

## Section C: Field-Level Discrepancies

### C1. Confirmed discrepancies requiring resolution

| Item | Field | BPC Value | Part 4 Value | Resolution |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A-09 | Vibration threshold | ISO 2631-1 values only; 0.1 m/s RMS NOT in ISO | A-09 cites "0.1 m/s RMS" | **OPEN** — UNVERIFIED flag must appear on A-09 before publication. BPC floor-vibration-wheelchair-disability §Critical Flag confirms this discrepancy. |
| A-02 | Acoustic criterion | BPC: STI is primary criterion; NRC is means not end | Part 4: NRC ≥0.85 as criterion | **OPEN** — RAP-22 (Amlani & Russo 2016) CONTRADICTED this: NRC-compliant panels can reduce STI. Part 4 A-02 specification should reference STI ≥0.60 as performance criterion; NRC ≥0.85 as procurement proxy only. |
| D-03 | Toilet visibility % | BPC: [UNVERIFIED-QUANT] 47% figure removed | Part 4: may reference 47% | Review Part 4 D-03 before publication — remove/flag any 47% quantified claim. |
| E-10 | Rest seating interval | BPC: 25–30m (Tier 1 evidence, Roxburgh 2024) | Part 4: 20m (first entry, now redirected); 25m (canonical) | RESOLVED — canonical E-10 at L3205 uses 25m. Redirect in place. |
| G-03 | Grab bar type | BPC: vertical/bilateral preferred (Kennedy 2015); horizontal ADA = least preferred | Part 4 G-03: "Clinical Positioning and Bilateral" in title — verify spec body matches BPC finding | Review G-03 spec body — confirm vertical bar provision is primary, not solely horizontal. |
| B-01 | Circadian metric | BPC (therapeutic-lighting-design): EML ≥150–200; BPC (circadian-lighting): melanopic EDI ≥250 | Part 4 B-01: "≥150 EML" in title | **DISCREPANCY** — per project-standards Opus note 2026-04-09, use melanopic EDI ≥250 (Brown et al. 2022) as unified target. B-01 title and specification should be updated to melanopic EDI ≥250. |

### C2. Items where BPC confidence disclosure is required in Part 4

The following Part 4 items must carry an evidence-level disclosure before publication, per project-standards standing rules:

| Item | Required disclosure |
|---|---|
| All F-series | OFS/PAIN provisions: zero built-environment spatial parameters exist in any jurisdiction [per pain-ofs BPC] |
| A-09 | 0.1 m/s RMS threshold UNVERIFIED — source required before publication |
| B-01 | Metric discrepancy: update from EML to melanopic EDI ≥250 |
| D-03 | Remove/flag any quantified incontinence reduction % |
| All NDV items | Tier 4–5 evidence only; no RCT-level thresholds validated [per ndv-aut BPC] |
| K-01–K-04 | Novel specifications; DBL specialist verification required at Stage 3 |

## Section D: BPC Slugs Missing from Slug Registry

All 70 BPC files migrated in Block 2 are accounted for in the slug registry or are merged/redirect stubs. No orphaned BPC files identified.

**Three deferred non-standard files** (not migrated in Block 2 — require decision before Block 5 parser validation):
- `bathroom-typology-global-south` (bathrooms-and-wet-areas)
- `fold-down-grab-bar-specification` (bathrooms-and-wet-areas)
- `bariatric-turning-radius-built-environment` (seating-and-rest)
